title: EC under the microscope
desc: Allegations of irregularities in the handling of the general election by the Election Commission (EC) have drawn widespread attention in the country’s unfolding constitutional debate.

title: Tourism meets culture
desc: A proposal to merge Thailand's culture and tourism portfolios to strengthen the country's cultural "soft power" could boost high-value tourism, but bureaucratic reform and greater private-sector involvement will be crucial for the strategy to succeed, according to a former tourism minister.

title: Coal units restarted to curb electricity costs
desc: The Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC) has ordered the restart of two decommissioned units at the Mae Moh coal-fired power plant in a bid to contain rising electricity bills.

title: Rising fuel costs force fishing fleets to dock
desc: Fishing fleets nationwide are halting operations as surging fuel prices and shortages push costs beyond sustainable levels.

title: Ex-MPs 'guilty' in ethical cases
desc: The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) has found two former MPs guilty of serious ethical breaches in separate gambling and land cases.

title: Fuel frustration mounts
desc: Public frustration is mounting across Thailand over restricted access to fuel, despite repeated government assurances that national reserves remain ample.

title: Thailand seeks Iran's help for safety of seafarers
desc: Thailand has asked Iran to assist three Thai crew members aboard the Mayuree Naree and to ensure the safe passage of Thai merchant ships through the Strait of Hormuz, amidst escalating conflict in the Middle East.

title: Getting a handle on the oil confusion
desc: An oil shortage at some petrol stations over the past week raised doubts as it contradicts the government's assurances of a stable fuel supply.

title: Mideast conflict threatens Thai growth
desc: The Bank of Thailand is warning the war in the Middle East could weigh on Thailand's economy across multiple fronts, potentially dragging GDP growth below its 2% forecast for this year.

title: Tourism groups eye B15bn stimulus
desc: Seven tourism associations under the Federation of Thai Tourism Associations (Fetta) have called for stimulus projects worth a combined 15 billion baht, including cash handouts to local tourists, to prevent an industry downturn due to the Gulf war.

title: Motorists say ‘Price doesn’t matter, just let us fill up’ as fuel queues stretch in Sri Racha
desc: Motorists line up for more than a kilometer to refill their tanks at a Thai Oil fuel station in Sri Racha, Chonburi, after word spread that the station was allowing fuel purchases without strict limits.

title: Clash among transgender groups over missing phone draws crowds of tourists at Walking Street Pattaya
desc: Two groups of transgender individuals confront each other near the entrance of Walking Street Pattaya early on March 20 over a dispute involving a missing mobile phone.

title: Foreign man found naked and shouting on Phra Tamnak Road in Pattaya, police seek to identify relatives
desc: Authorities detain a foreign man found walking naked and shouting along Phra Tamnak Road in Pattaya early on March 20.

title: German tourist injured after falling from songthaew on Pattaya Beach Road
desc: A female tourist from Germany receives first aid after reportedly falling from a songthaew on Pattaya Beach Road on March 20.

title: Pattaya reviews city development plan, sets long-term vision for quality urban growth
desc: Deputy Mayor Manot Nongyai chairs a Pattaya City Development Committee meeting at City Hall on March 19 to review revisions to the city’s development plan.

title: Viral clip shows tourist threatened, robbed in Patong
desc: PHUKET — 21 March 2026, A CCTV clip showing a foreign tourist being threatened and assaulted before having his cash stolen in Patong has been widely shared.

title: Severe weather warning issued for 40 Thai provinces
desc: Thailand’s Meteorological Department has warned of severe weather tomorrow, with thunderstorms, hail, and strong winds forecast across 40 provinces.

title: Man punches another over urination on Pattaya street, drawing calls for public respect
desc: A street confrontation in the tourist city of Pattaya has triggered a heated debate online after a viral video showed a man punching another man over alleged public urination.

title: Finland the world’s happiest country nine years running with insights for Thailand
desc: Finland has been named the happiest country in the world for the ninth consecutive year, with the report offering insights for Thailand.

title: Hospitals limit drug dispensing
desc: Three major hospitals have adjusted outpatient drug dispensing as a precaution to prevent potential shortages amid energy-related disruptions linked to the Middle East crisis.

title: Police cuff two monks over rapes
desc: Police arrested two monks for allegedly raping seven novices and filming the abuse after luring the victims to their quarters.

title: Rural school funding reforms piloted
desc: The Equitable Education Fund (EEF) is piloting a new budget allocation formula to support small, remote schools, aiming to improve access, efficiency and student outcomes.

title: Eastern destinations ride out flight disruption fallout
desc: Tourism operators along Thailand's eastern coast say the region is largely insulated from the flight disruptions currently affecting southern destinations.

title: Acer looks beyond computer market
desc: Acer Computer is accelerating its expansion beyond the computer market into healthcare, energy, industrial PCs and large home appliances, while stepping up local assembly to expand its "Made in Thailand" portfolio.

title: Volatility easing as traders adjust to war
desc: Trade on Thai and global stock markets has been volatile this month amid escalating conflicts in the Middle East. This week, attacks on energy infrastructure by both the Israeli-US and Iranian sides are having a direct impact on energy prices and availability.

title: Central Retail combines 3 food companies
desc: Central Retail Corporation Plc (CRC) has integrated its retail and wholesale businesses across Thailand and Vietnam, appointing Stephane Coum as chief executive to oversee the entire food business portfolio.

title: Ministry preps tax schemes for SMEs
desc: The Finance Ministry is preparing tax reduction measures for large businesses that help bring small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) into their supply chains, in a bid to help upgrade local SMEs.

title: A wander through pink hotel rooms
desc: We've all been there. You've paced all seven floors, had coffee breaks until you're full and stared at enough mannequins to last a lifetime. Bangkok moves at a rapid speed, and sometimes the "mall crawl" leaves you craving a quiet corner and a chance to actually make something rather than just buy it.

title: Russian flair
desc: In honour of Her Royal Highness Princess Sirivannavari Nariratana Rajakanya and in tribute to her distinguished genius in arts and culture, the Ministry of Culture in collaboration with the Russian embassy and the Royal Bangkok Symphony Orchestra (RBSO) Foundation, supported by B.Grimm, recently presented a spectacular concert titled Russian Seasons at the Thailand Cultural Centre (TCC).

title: Why we need walkable cities
desc: Morning work commutes in Bangkok are tackled like olympian tasks. For some, it's a trudge down a narrow soi, followed by a motorbike taxi serpentining through traffic, leading to a transfer onto the BTS or MRT. Especially ambitious commutes may end with a walk across a skywalk or through a shopping complex before reaching the office. What looks like a straightforward commute on a map instead...

title: Finding the soul of a city
desc: Cannes and Grasse in France, and Kumamoto and Hokkaido in Japan are examples of cities with creative city branding. These unique cities inspired the creation of the project "Neramyth City: Branding Cities Into Creative Assets".

title: Love in time of censorship
desc: Women negotiate with China's repression of queer romance through consumption of Thai boys' love (BL), according to research by Xiaoping Wu.

title: Lufthansa Group Executives visit children at HHN Foundation in Pattaya, supporting youth development projects
desc: Executives from Lufthansa Group visit children at the HHN Foundation for Thai Children in Central Pattaya, sharing a special lunch and activities that brought smiles to the youngsters. PATTAYA, Thailand – Senior executives from Lufthansa Group visited children under the care of the HHN Foundation for Thai Children in Central Pattaya on March 17, bringing smiles and support while reinforcing the company’s commitment to youth development initiatives in the local community. The delegation was led by Felipe Bonifatti, Vice President Asia Pacific, Middle East & Joint Ventures East of Lufthansa

title: Thai baht strengthens to 32.49 per dollar as global tensions ease
desc: The Thai baht strengthened to around 32.49 per US dollar in early trading, supported by a weaker dollar and easing concerns over geopolitical tensions, while markets continue to monitor Thailand’s export outlook and global economic developments. PATTAYA, Thailand – The Thai baht strengthened to 32.49 per US dollar in early trading on Friday, gaining from the previous close of 32.82, as the US dollar weakened amid easing concerns over tensions in the Middle East. Krungthai Bank currency strategist Poon Panichpibool said the baht’s appreciation largely followed the decline in the US Dollar Index

title: Hot and breezy days with occasional showers in Pattaya as summer continues
desc: On a Pattaya baht bus, foreign visitors explore the city, making the most of every sunny or rainy moment. (Photo by Jetsada Homklin) PATTAYA, Thailand – Tourists and locals in Pattaya are enjoying sunny mornings, but should stay prepared for brief afternoon thunderstorms, according to the Thai Meteorological Department. The upper part of Thailand remains under high heat pressure, while southeast winds carry moisture from the Gulf of Thailand, causing isolated showers in the region. For Pattaya and nearby Chonburi, there’s a 10% chance of thunderstorms, with daytime highs around 33–37°C and ove

title: Fuel shortage thinning Suvarnabhumi taxi ranks
desc: A fuel shortage is severely disrupting taxi services at Suvarnabhumi Airport, with large SUV taxis gradually suspending operations and only about 2,500 vehicles currently in active service, out of a fleet estimated at more than 5,000, operators say.

title: Two Bangkok monks charged with abusing novices
desc: Two monks have been arrested at a well-known temple in the Charoen Krung area of Bangkok on charges of sexual abusing novice monks, with seven victims identified and over 100 explicit video clips seized as evidence.

title: Police send ‘VIP runway’ case to anti-graft body
desc: Police have completed their investigation into a “VIP runway” on land owned by the family of Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul in Nakhon Ratchasima and forwarded the case to the National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC).

title: Guru's Weekly Buzz: March 20-26
desc: Guru By Bangkok Post's weekly pick of the most exciting products, activities, food and travel to indulge in.

title: Thai women among most favourite for Koreans to tie the knot
desc: Women from Thailand have emerged as one of the top foreigners getting married to South Koreans.

title: US transfers 17 Stryker vehicles to Thai army during Hanuman Guardian 2026
desc: LOPBURI — The United States Army Pacific (USARPAC) has ceremonially transferred 17 Stryker armoured vehicles to the Royal Thai Army during the closing ceremony of Exercise Hanuman Guardian 2026, marking a new step in bilateral defence cooperation.

title: Living Beyond Boundaries in Paradise
desc: The mesmerizing Andaman Sea stretching endlessly and emerald green hills are the perfect backdrop for stepping into lifestyle-led luxury living at The Residences at InterContinental Phuket Resort.

title: 2 injured, southern human rights MP unharmed in ambush
desc: NARATHIWAT - Prachachat Party MP Kamonsak Leewamoh narrowly escaped an attack by gunmen as he was returning to his home in Bacho district in the early hours of Friday.

title: Wild elephant raids kitchen, devours rice in Chachoengsao
desc: CHACHOENGSAO — 19 March 2026, A wild elephant broke into a rural home in eastern Thailand overnight, rummaging through a kitchen and eating stored rice, officials said on Friday.

title: Finland tops World Happiness Report in 2026, Thailand slips 3 places
desc: Finland tops World Happiness Report in 2026, Thailand slips 3 places Legacy Finland has been ranked the world’s happiest country for a ninth consecutive year in the World Happiness Report 2026 , while Thailand fell three places.

title: Thai man rapes and blackmails ex-girlfriend over unpaid debt
desc: Thai man rapes and blackmails ex-girlfriend over unpaid debt Legacy A Thai man surrendered to Bangkok police after he raped his ex-girlfriend in a public bathroom and later blackmailed her.

title: Naked foreign man detained after Pattaya street disturbance
desc: PATTAYA — 20 March 2026, A foreign man was detained after walking naked and shouting along a road in Pattaya in the early hours of Friday, alarming residents and motorists.

title: Mum on the run in Asia TikToker jailed after return to UK from Thailand
desc: A British TikToker known online as “ Mum on the run in Asia ” was sentenced to 26 months in prison for drug offences after returning to the UK from Thailand.

title: From fashion to private jets: New luxury ecosystem shapes Thailand’s high-end travel landscape
desc: Luxury in Thailand is evolving beyond traditional markers of wealth such as hotels, shopping and real estate, as lifestyle brands, mobility and curated experiences increasingly shape how high-net-worth travellers engage with the destination.

title: Krabi police seize drugs, cache of weapons
desc: KRABI — 20 March 2026, Police in Krabi have arrested members of a drug trafficking network and seized a large cache of firearms, including military-style weapons, during a province-wide crackdown.

title: Celebrate Songkran 2026 in Southern Thailand with these beachfront stays in Phuket and Khao Lak
desc: Three Marriott resorts in southern Thailand are promoting Songkran 2026 stays and in-resort celebrations, featuring water-themed festivities and seasonal dining.

title: Chinese man blacklisted for filing fake theft to Chiang Mai police
desc: A Chinese man is suspected of filing a fake theft complaint in Chiang Mai in an attempt to obtain a police document for an insurance claim before leaving Thailand.

title: FUSO Thailand reshapes organisational structure with new CEO and CFO appointments, accelerating full-force commercial vehicle strategy
desc: FUSO Commercial Vehicles (Thailand) Ltd. (FCVT) has announced a significant corporate restructuring and appointed new Thai senior executives to drive brand strategy.

title: Foreign woman attacked by transwomen on Phuket’s Bangla Road
desc: A group of Thai transwomen physically assaulted a foreign woman on Bangla Road in Patong, Phuket, after the foreigner allegedly caused a disturbance.

title: 5 things to do this weekend in Bangkok (March 21 to 22)
desc: A roundup of activities in Bangkok including retro-style markets in Siam, the planetarium, and events in Lumpini Park.

title: SRT Red Line Salaya–Nakhon Pathom extension mapped for 2033 opening
desc: Thailand’s State Railway of Thailand (SRT) is advancing plans for the Red Line Salaya–Nakhon Pathom extension, a 25.3-kilometre route with construction targets starting in 2026.

title: Netherlands Embassy Bangkok relocation set for August
desc: Netherlands Embassy Bangkok relocation set for August Legacy The Embassy of the Netherlands in Thailand announced it will relocate its Bangkok office to Dusit Central Park in August , ending its long-standing presence on Wireless Road, one of the city’s prime areas. The embassy is currently based on a 32,000-square-metre plot in Soi Tonson off Wireless Road (Witthayu Road), an area known for its greenery and concentration of major businesses and diplomatic missions.
